title: Emirates flight from Glasgow Airport to Dubai cancelled

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A flight from Glasgow Airport has been cancelled after the United States and Israel launched military strikes on Iran.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Glasgow Airport flight cancelled after U.S and Israel attack Iran (Image: Newsquest) This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Emirates flight EK028 to Dubai International has been cancelled after the General Civil Aviation Authority announced the temporary partial closure of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) airspace. The flight had been due to depart at 1.35pm and check-in had opened at the airport.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Glasgow Airport flight cancelled after U.S and Israel attack Iran
